title: rubus incultus var. viridis
intro: Rubus incultus var. viridis, also known as the wild raspberry, is a type of bramble that belongs to the rose family and is characterized by its greenish-yellow flowers and edible fruit. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Rubus incultus var. viridis include its ability to lower blood sugar levels, reduce the risk of heart disease, and aid in the prevention of certain types of cancer. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and jams, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
